title: Beatmatching by Ear
description: Learn to sync two tracks purely by listening — hear the drift, feel the lock, adjust without looking at BPM numbers.

requiredTracks.characteristics: tracks with clear, punchy kick drums — house or techno

steps.instruction: Watch: both decks play with a slight BPM difference. Listen — you can hear the kicks flam and drift apart.
Watch: the pitch fader adjusts slowly. The flam narrows. When it disappears, the BPMs match.
Watch: even with matching BPMs, the beats drift over time. The jog wheel nudges Deck B to stay in phase.

phases.instruction: Close your eyes. Hear the kick of Deck A — boom. Now hear Deck B's kick slightly offset. Imagine reaching for the pitch fader and slowing Deck B down just enough until both kicks sound like one single punch. That click of alignment is what you are chasing.

steps.instruction: Cover the BPM display if you can. You are training your ears today. Play both decks and just listen.
Listen: are the kicks flaming? If Deck B sounds like it is rushing ahead, you need to slow it down — push the pitch fader slightly up.
Make your first adjustment. Nudge the pitch fader in the direction you think is correct. Listen for the flam to narrow.
Getting closer. Smaller adjustments now. The closer you get, the flam will slow down — like two pulses aligning into one.
Last mile. Tiny pitch tweaks only. When you hear a single, clean kick instead of two — you are matched.
Now check phase. Even with matching BPMs, the kicks might not land together. Nudge the jog wheel forward or backward to snap them into lock.
Hold the lock for 16 beats. Keep your ear on the phase — if drift creeps in, a tiny nudge corrects it.
You matched by ear. Now glance at the BPM display — how close did you get? This is how pros train.
